reason: Modifying /etc/fstab to add a mount option changes the filesystem configuration. An error in the fstab entry can prevent /home from mounting, leading to loss of user data access and potentially breaking SSH or console logins. Because this is a partitioning/filesystem change, the policy classifies it as too dangerous to remediate automatically.
estimated_impact: reboot required; filesystems affected; Boot failure if fstab syntax is incorrect; Loss of access to user home directories; Potential SSH login failures; System may require reboot to apply change
